title: mangifera laurina
intro: Mangifera laurina, also known as the West African mango or horse mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its evergreen leaves and small, yellowish fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Mangifera laurina include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of malaria. Herbal preparations of Mangifera laurina can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
